title: Performance-budget test layer — pytest-benchmark assertions with regression-alert baselines
summary: Perf-budget layer shipped as designed. S01-S10 cycle count = 0 (cleanest in Phase 4 sample). S11 (integration-tests) burned 8 fix cycles on pre-existing test-fixture brittleness pulled into CR-00083's blast radius by a scope amendment.

findings.title: _compute_dirty_count() lazy import requires triple monkeypatch in every dashboard route test
findings.evidence: ai-dev/active/CR-00083/fix-cycles/CR-00083_S11_FIX_cycle{1..8}_prompt.md — 8 cycles chasing same 6 tests. Root cause finally pinned to dashboard/routers/worktrees.py:_compute_dirty_count's `from orch.db.session import get_session as _gs` lazy import bypassing the test's _engine/_session_local module patches because get_session internally calls _get_session_local() by name and the test wasn't patching that third function. Fix: also monkeypatch _get_session_local. Filed in all four affected test fixtures.
findings.recommendation: Refactor _compute_dirty_count to accept a Session factory as a constructor/argument so tests don't need to monkeypatch a triple of module-level names. Or, at minimum, document the triple-patch pattern in tests/CLAUDE.md.

findings.title: OSS background thread holds SessionLocal beyond testcontainer teardown
findings.evidence: S11 QvGate report tail shows PytestUnhandledThreadExceptionWarning from dashboard/routers/oss.py:495's lambda: asyncio.run(_run_oss_job(job.id)) — the thread captures SessionLocal at module load and survives the test's testcontainer teardown, producing 'connection refused at port 51396' warnings on every integration run. Not the cause of the 6 failed tests, but noise that confused 4–5 of the 8 fix cycles.
findings.recommendation: OSS thread should accept a Session factory from its caller, and the dashboard's TestClient fixture should track + join any spawned threads before tearing down the engine.

findings.title: Fix-cycle budget exhaustion doesn't distinguish flake vs. real bug
findings.evidence: All 8 S11 cycles chased the same 6 tests with same root cause; daemon kept producing fix-cycle prompts until the budget cap, then surrendered. No signal that 'identical failure set + identical previous fix attempt = pivot to deeper diagnosis (e.g., add tracebacks, run subset in isolation)'.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'failure-set fingerprint' to fix_cycle.py: if cycle N's failure set is identical to cycle N-1's, escalate the prompt with a deeper-diagnosis preamble (run failing tests in isolation, capture full traceback, look upstream).

findings.title: 10-round baseline + 50% headroom held up on first real use
findings.evidence: S02 daemon poll-loop: σ/μ = 0.93 → switched to min (BUDGET_MS = 9, raised to 44 for NullPool). S03 RAG: σ/μ = 0.048 → mean (BUDGET_S = 0.04 s). S03 routes: all σ/μ < 0.3 → p50 per route. No mid-step budget revision; final test passed on first attempt for all 7 perf tests. Phase 4 roadmap can keep the 10-round methodology unchanged.

findings.title: S01-split (deps/marker only) prevented perf-marker wiring failure mode
findings.evidence: S07-S10 (lint, assertions, format, typecheck, unit-tests) all collected default test sets without picking up tests/perf/**. The 'addopts and not perf' + conftest auto-marker pattern held up on first contact. The CR-00076 step-granularity lesson (extract setup into a tiny self-verifying step) earned its keep.

test_summary: S06 lint, S07 assertions, S08 format-check, S09 typecheck, S10 unit-tests, S11 integration-tests (after manual takeover), S12 diff-coverage (98%), S13 security-secrets — all PASS.
notes: Analysis completed; 5 findings recorded (1 medium, 2 low, 2 info). CR-00083 ships as designed. The S11 fix-cycle explosion is the only signal that materially differs from prior Phase 4 CRs — root-caused to pre-existing brittleness in dashboard test fixtures, not the perf modules themselves.
